messages.author: Arthur - Dept 44 - S5
messages.text: Hey meshers, would anyone be motivated to hack a meshcore chat application on #PalmOS devices ? The one on the photo has a nice display, nice keyboard and even Bluetooth! Avoiding the landfill for these devices (also with the phasing out of 2G/3G), would be an awesome reuse of resources . Looking into Palm SDK and existing open source apps for this platform...
messages.content_html: Hey meshers, would anyone be motivated to hack a meshcore chat application on #PalmOS devices ? The one on the photo has a nice display, nice keyboard and even Bluetooth! Avoiding the landfill for these devices (also with the phasing out of 2G/3G), would be an awesome reuse of resources . Looking into Palm SDK and existing open source apps for this platform...

messages.author: Skye
messages.text: That would be cool, how do you load software onto it?
messages.content_html: That would be cool, how do you load software onto it?

messages.author: Skye
messages.text: I thought about getting a blackberry key2 to test as the app supports very old android but they're not cheap
messages.content_html: I thought about getting a blackberry key2 to test as the app supports very old android but they're not cheap

messages.author: Arthur - Dept 44 - S5
messages.text: applications can be loaded via an sdcard or via cable and some software (hotsync)
messages.content_html: applications can be loaded via an sdcard or via cable and some software (hotsync)

messages.author: Skye
messages.text: Looks like webos is Linux based, should be possible? Not sure about Bluetooth min requirements
messages.content_html: Looks like webos is Linux based, should be possible? Not sure about Bluetooth min requirements

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: Unfortunelty the bluetooth stack is too old. But soon something new is comming in the next days/week I can write it here, when I finish with the documentation
messages.content_html: Unfortunelty the bluetooth stack is too old. But soon something new is comming in the next days/week I can write it here, when I finish with the documentation

messages.author: Skye
messages.text: Ah too bad. These radios all use BLE or BT5 it seems. Serial might be an option (edited)
messages.content_html: Ah too bad. These radios all use BLE or BT5 it seems. Serial might be an option (edited)

messages.author: Arthur - Dept 44 - S5
messages.text: @gdob/LZ1WOW is there not a firmware way to serve older bluetooth stacks with the hardware commonly used for meshcore ?
messages.content_html: @gdob/LZ1WOW is there not a firmware way to serve older bluetooth stacks with the hardware commonly used for meshcore ?

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: I did a research and I found that meshcore fw needs to be changed in order to use bluetooth classic / Bluetooth Serial. Most of the old phone supported Bluetooth 2.0. The problem here is that bluetooth serial only will work on esp32. Unforunetly on nrf52840 is not possible to simulate older blueetoth classic or bluetooth serial. Its hardware limitation. So I decide to use for my project wifi tcp. I can provide more details soon. (edited)
messages.content_html: I did a research and I found that meshcore fw needs to be changed in order to use bluetooth classic / Bluetooth Serial. Most of the old phone supported Bluetooth 2.0. The problem here is that bluetooth serial only will work on esp32. Unforunetly on nrf52840 is not possible to simulate older blueetoth classic or bluetooth serial. Its hardware limitation. So I decide to use for my project wifi tcp. I can provide more details soon. (edited)
